title: Quick action vs workflow
summary: Know that quick actions are focused repeatable operations, while workflows coordinate broader multi-step process logic.
answer: Use quick actions for targeted actions; use workflows for multi-step orchestration.

title: Incident vs problem
summary: Incidents restore service now; problems focus on root cause and recurrence reduction.
answer: Different process goals, timelines, and outputs.

title: BYOD vs corporate-owned
summary: Ownership changes policy scope, privacy expectations, and app/control strategy.
answer: Anchor your answer in governance and user experience.

title: Discovery vs ITAM record
summary: Discovery finds and normalizes what exists; ITAM governs lifecycle, ownership, and financial context.

title: Patch ring vs broad rollout
summary: Patch rings reduce risk through staged validation; broad rollout trades speed for more risk.
answer: Always frame this as risk-aware deployment.

title: Saved search vs dashboard
summary: Saved searches are reusable filtered datasets; dashboards are presentation surfaces using those datasets.

title: Knowledge article vs workaround
summary: A workaround may become part of knowledge, but knowledge must be curated, reviewed, and reusable.
answer: Operational hint vs governed support content.

title: Compliance state vs remediation action
summary: State evaluation marks a device non-compliant; remediation defines what happens next.

title: iPaaS connector vs custom integration
summary: Connectors accelerate low-code delivery, but custom logic may still be needed for special mapping or control.

title: Workspace context vs ticket notes
summary: Workspace context unifies device/user/app/service signals, while notes are manual record content.
answer: Live operational context vs human-authored record history.
